item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs editing for awkward phrasing, some unstated details that I feel like the foundation overlooked, etc. On mobile rn tho

item.title: Re: i didn't pick a dear hunter song for authpost but it's still real nice.
item.content:encoded: Emotions aren't a physical object capable of what you're suggesting. I have had plenty occasions where I feel angry and I simply stop and the feelings do just go away. The mind is much more complex than a bottle holding fluids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be entirely fair, The Littlest Browbeater only got so low because it was in rewrite for over a year before kaktus stepped in and rewrote it himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just because a series 1 article like 999 can't be reimagined into something different because it's mired in its past is kind of saying that "this thing is so bad, nobody can remake it into something better/interesting."

Which, I believe, does the author a disservice of trying to do something new and different with an old article. Just because an article sucks and is rich in the history of the site, doesn't automatically mean that nobody can do anything with it. That restrains creativity just because it's so bad. That's like saying kaktus can't do his own version of The Littlest Browbeater because it's so universally disliked by the community that he can't try to fix it.

I don't think the fact that this article is based around Series I SCPs takes anything away from it. This article adds a nice, new take on an old SCP that could use a little more than just "an orange slimeball that cures depression". The idea that 999 doesn't just cure depression but rather whisks it away is pretty neat.

This article does have an odd layer of ambiguity to it that confuses me a little, like why does the farmhouse have a 20-meter-radius "negative zone" or why is there a room with the sounds of whimpering dogs? But I suppose that's what you intended.

For the paragraph about room four you have "A prototype SEACM" but in earlier paragraphs you wrote it as "SEA-CM", you may want to fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is the best DoA article not counting SCP-4220. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given the SCP that this is implied to be the birthplace of, I feel this is far too melodramatic.

+ My thoughts
- My thoughts

Both 999 and 682 are old Series 1 shlock, but 999 especially. Having a dark, mysterious take on 999's creation doesn't change the fact that 999 fucking blows, and this article honestly suffers by trying to do anything with it.

Additionally, a happiness-generating slimeball is nowhere near effective enough at what it does to be considered the antithesis to something like 682.

Hard -1.

item.title: i didn't pick a dear hunter song for authpost but it's still real nice.
item.content:encoded:
Like this piece? See it and many more saplings like it, at Popsioak's Garden, here!

Formless shapes in the darkness
We are as harmless as the thoughts in our heads
Drinking poison and chewing on concrete
leaving burn holes in the sheets of our beds
we think the world is alright
we think the world is alright, and that's a lie

Thanks to Connor MacWarren, TrustyOlValet2, BlueJones, Brewsterion 1017. Yossipossi for critique!
Image Creds:

https://pxhere.com/en/photo/1369212 - Public Domain.

Spoilers

+ show block
– hide block

This is where 999 was made. The "shadow" 682 is a conglomeration of all the anger, rage, anxiety, sadness, and negativity it's whisked away. Just like energy, it can't be created or destroyed — only bottled up deep inside you, like any emotion. Only thing is — it's doomed to explode later.
